title: psilurus incurvus var. hirtellus
intro: Psilurus incurvus var. hirtellus, commonly known as Hirtella, is a species of plant in the family Olacaceae. Hirtella is a medicinal plant used in traditional medicine, particularly in South America, where it is valued for its various health benefits. The plant has been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for wound healing and treating infections. The bioactive constituents of Hirtella include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic actions, such as analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects.
